evals.name: feature-plan-from-description
evals.prompt: Plan the implementation for adding webhook support to our notification system. We need to let users register webhook URLs, validate them with a test ping, and deliver event payloads with retries on failure. The codebase already has email and SMS notification channels.
evals.expected_output: A docs/plans/webhook-support/ folder with plan.md overview, phase-N.md files for each logical capability, DOT dependency graph, codebase context section, and interactive Q&A before phase design
expectations: Announces 'Using the planning skill to create an implementation plan' at the start
Explores the codebase before designing phases — reads existing notification channel files, traces data flow through email/SMS implementations
Uses Agent sub-agents or Glob/Grep/Read to investigate the existing codebase patterns
Asks clarifying questions using the AskUserQuestion tool before designing phases — not embedded in plain text
All questions are resolved before proceeding to phase design (hard gate respected)
Creates a docs/plans/ directory structure with plan.md and at least two phase-N.md files
plan.md contains a DOT digraph showing phase dependencies
plan.md contains a Codebase Context section with Existing Patterns to Follow
Each phase-N.md has a clear Overview, Implementation section with Files, What to test, and Done When criteria
anti_expectations: Does not skip codebase exploration and jump straight to writing phase documents
Does not embed questions in plain text output instead of using AskUserQuestion tool
Does not design phases before resolving all open questions from the Q&A step
Does not omit the DOT dependency digraph from plan.md

evals.name: plan-from-design-doc-with-parallel-phases
evals.prompt: I have a design doc at docs/spec/search-indexing.md that describes adding full-text search to our product catalog. It covers indexing strategy, query parsing, and result ranking. Please create an implementation plan from this design doc.
evals.expected_output: A multi-phase plan under docs/plans/search-indexing/ derived from the design doc, with independent phases identified for parallel execution, codebase exploration findings, and user Q&A completed before phase design
expectations: Announces 'Using the planning skill to create an implementation plan' at the start
Reads the design document thoroughly before exploring the codebase
Explores the codebase to find existing patterns, test infrastructure, and potential conflicts
Uses multiple-choice format when asking clarifying questions via AskUserQuestion
plan.md references the design doc as the source in the header metadata
plan.md includes Acceptance Criteria derived from the design doc
DOT digraph correctly shows independent phases with no edges between them (parallelizable)
Each phase delivers a recognizable capability — not just 'step 1' or 'part 1'
anti_expectations: Does not skip reading the design document and invent requirements
Does not create phases that leave the codebase in a broken state with failing tests
Does not ask questions embedded in plain text instead of using AskUserQuestion tool

evals.name: fix-tag-plan-update
evals.prompt: Update the existing plan at docs/plans/auth-system/. I've added @fix tags to phase-2.md and phase-3.md marking what needs to change. Phase 2 is too large and needs splitting, and phase 3 should use argon2 instead of bcrypt.
evals.expected_output: Updated phase files with @fix tags processed and removed, phase-2 split into smaller phases, phase-3 updated to use argon2, plan.md updated to reflect structural changes
expectations: Reads all phase files in the plan directory to find @fix tags
Collects all @fix tags and summarizes the changes to the user before applying
Splits phase-2 into multiple smaller phase files when the @fix tag indicates it is too large
Updates phase-3 content to replace bcrypt with argon2 as directed by the @fix tag
Removes all @fix tags from the phase files after applying the changes
Updates plan.md to reflect the new phase structure including any new phases from splitting
Updates the DOT digraph in plan.md to include the new phase nodes and dependencies
Uses AskUserQuestion to present the updated plan summary for approval
anti_expectations: Does not leave @fix tags in the files after processing them
Does not update phase files without also updating plan.md to match the new structure
Does not apply changes silently without summarizing them to the user first
